raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Businesses can reduce employees’ hours and wages as an alternative to layoffs through California’s unemployment insurance Work Share Program.",
    "Eligible workers receive prorated unemployment benefits to replace lost wages under the Work Share Program.",
    "Participation in the Work Share Program helps businesses retain valuable, trained workers.",
    "The application process for the Work Share Program has been widely underutilized because it can be slow.",
    "Expedited processing of Work Share applications could prevent unnecessary layoffs.",
    "Workers are more likely to accept part-time jobs if their unemployment benefits are not significantly offset by their wages.",
    "Relationships between businesses and their workers are critical to the quality and speed of economic recovery.",
    "AB 1731 would create a temporary, simplified work-sharing application process requiring EDD to approve applications upon receipt.",
    "EDD is instructed to send employers their claim packets electronically within 5 days after the application is approved.",
    "Increasing outreach to employers can help to overcome hurdles to expanding the Work Share program.",
    "The federal CARES Act reimburses states for Work Share benefit payments."
  ]
}
```
